description: Georgian uvular ejective may range allophonically from a highly-constricted, low-flow uvular fricative to an ejective uvular stop (with varying degrees of frication at offset) to a glottal stop. This represents continuous variation within the uvular ejective category.
sources: Shosted, R. K., & Chikovani, M. (2006). _Standard Georgian_. Berkeley Phonology Lab.
Vicenik, C. (2010). _An Acoustic Study of Georgian Stop Consonants_. University of California, Berkeley.
